course_description:
This course provides students with the opportunity to conduct their own research project. Through this course students will develop important research skills such as systematically reviewing the literature, safe laboratory and field work practices, proper record keeping, analysis and interpretation of results, and presentation of scientific ideas in writing. The purpose of this course is to allow students to develop a deeper understanding of a subject that interests them, and to provide students with research and project management experience that can be applied beyond this course. There is no lecture, practical, or tutorial offered in the course. Students will conduct research independently with the guidance of their supervisors. Supervisor availability is not guaranteed, as supervision of research projects is in demand. Students are advised to contact potential supervisors well in advance of the semester in which they plan to enrol. A research topic approval form must be submitted before enrolment can be completed. See https://environment.uq.edu.au/current-students/apply-research-project or contact School for details.

This is a research-based course where a student investigates in detail a topic defined by the student in conjunction with their academic supervisor. The purpose of this course is to allow students to develop a deeper understanding of key issues in the fields of their studies that interest them. Through this course students will develop important research skills such as systematically reviewing the literature, safe laboratory and field work practices, proper record keeping, analysis and interpretation of results, and presentation of scientific ideas in writing. The purpose of this course is to allow students to develop a deeper understanding of a subject that interests them, and to provide students with research and project management experience that can be applied beyond this course.

There is no lecture, practical, or tutorial offered in the course. Students will conduct research independently with the guidance of their supervisors. Supervisor availability is not guaranteed, as supervision of research projects is in demand. Students are advised to contact potential supervisors well in advance of the semester in which they plan to enrol.

It is the responsibility of the student to identify a supervisor with expertise in their chosen topic and negotiate details of the research project with the supervisor. Prior to the semester of commencement and once the student and supervisor agree on a topic, students will need to submit a completed special topics permission form online in order to obtain permission to enrol. The form is available atﾠ https://environment.uq.edu.au/current-students/apply-honours-or-research-project .

*Make sure you have selected the correct course. The School offers a range of courses carrying different unit values (which reflect the scale and scope of the research) and durations. Read the information at https://environment.uq.edu.au/envm-coded-research-courses-postgraduate-students to choose the most suitable research course.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
Additional timetable information
There are no timetabled classes for this course. Contact with your academic supervisor should be discussed and arranged during your initial supervision discussions.
Guidance on workloads: As a four (4) Unit course, it would be expected that a workload of around 20 hours per week of self-directed learning would be the norm, for a semester workload.
course_aims:
This is a research-based course where students investigate in detail a topic of their interest.

The course aims are to:

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate enhanced content knowledge in the chosen research field.
Demonstrate enhanced data base access and interrogation skills (if applicable
Demonstrate enhanced field research skills (if applicable);
Demonstrate enhanced laboratory research skills (if applicable);
Demonstrate enhanced skills in critical thinking and/or logical analysis;
Demonstrate enhanced written and/or oral communication skills (as applicable).
Demonstrate skill in independently accessing relevant information resources

assessment_details.title: Proposal review
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a secure assessment, designed to ensure confidence that the work reflects the student’s own learning. In this assessment, students should demonstrate their understanding of the research topic, relevant literature, and the proposed methods for their research project to their supervisor(s). This assessment is also intended to provide timely feedback on the research proposal, to ensure the supervisor(s) and student agree on the research plan and timeline. The review consists of two parts: (1) Project proposal Students must submit a written proposal to their supervisor(s) before the meeting. This written proposal is not graded; therefore it can be in any format, any length, and can be a draft (discuss with research supervisors on their expectations). Writing the proposal is a way to get students prepared for the review meeting. The written proposal can also form part of the final report. As a guide (i.e. not a requirment), the proposal should be under 2,000 words (excluding references) and include: a. the background to the intended research; b. the research question, aims and objectives; c. the research methods; d. the expected outcomes; e. the significance of the research and its contribution to new knowledge; f. the timeline of the research (2) Review meeting held between weeks 4 and 6 The meeting should include: a. Student’s introduction and a presentation of their progress to date (suggested length: 5-10mins) b. A Q&A session for supervisor(s) to assess the student’s understanding of the topic, e.g. identifying the research gap(s), the appropriateness and limitations of methods, explaining the significance of the research, and outlining the next steps c. Feedback from the supervisor(s) on the proposal and discussion of any concerns The supervisor(s) will mark the student’s performance in the review meeting, based on their understanding of the project. The review meeting must be recorded, and a link of the recording should be submitted to Blackboard as proof of assessment completion. The meeting may be held online or in person (an in-person meeting can be recorded using a webcam/phone). The supervisor(s) may require the student to amend the proposal following the review. Students are responsible for organising the review meeting, recording the meeting, and completing a short form on Blackboard following the meeting. Students should start planning for the review early in the course, e.g. discuss with the supervisor on the meeting timing and their expectations. Submission guide: Students are to complete a short form on Blackboard following the meeting. The form includes a link (URL) to the recorded meeting. This should be completed and submitted by the due date. Late penalties apply. (As per UQ policy, the recording must be retained for at least one year after the release of final grades for potential re-marking). This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
An electronic form is available on Blackboard. Complete the form after the proposal review meeting with your supervisor and submit the form by the due date and time.
Please refer to Blackboard for the submission details.
Students is required to schedule their review meeting with their supervisor within week 4 to 6. Supervisor will provide marking for this assessment.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section further below for information relating to extension and deferral applications.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (the assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Final report
assessment_details.task_description: Details of the report format should be negotiated between the student and academic supervisor, in consideration of the marking criteria. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section further below for information relating to extension and deferral applications
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (the assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Research Performance
assessment_details.task_description: This is a secured assessment, designed to ensure confidence that the work reflects the student’s own learning. Students can fail this assessment if they are uncontactable by their supervisor(s) for extended periods without reason. The mark for research performance will be based on the supervisor's observations during the project, through regular communications, which may include in-person or online meetings, and email communications. The marking criteria include the student’s inquiry skills, discipline-specific skills, and professionalism. Examples of inquiry skills include, but are not limited to, gathering relevant scientific information using databases and library and the internet, extracting pertinent information from scientific papers, and participating in discussions with the supervisor and/or research group. Discipline-specific skills include, but are not limited to, generating data, collecting samples, and analysing data. Students are expected to consult with their supervisor about the skills required for their project. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
No submittable items for students. Supervisor to mark the student's research performance at the end of the semester.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
This item is not submittable by students, therefore no extensions or deferral are applicable.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .
learning_resources.other_course_materials: Recommended Item Description Further Requirement Network Share (J Drive) For research projects that require GIS capabilities, a new network share will be deployed in the same location as the UQ Digital Workspace (UQDW) desktops to improve performance. This brings the data closer to the virtual desktops and increases transfer performance for GIS applications and workflows.
learning_resources.recommended: Item Description Further Requirement Network Share (J Drive) For research projects that require GIS capabilities, a new network share will be deployed in the same location as the UQ Digital Workspace (UQDW) desktops to improve performance. This brings the data closer to the virtual desktops and increases transfer performance for GIS applications and workflows.

learning_activities.topic: Project This course is for self-directed learning in a topic approved by your supervisor. No formal classes are required; however contact expectations should be discussed with your supervisor. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
